summary: Merged a small dtk-template tooling update into melee. The only final file change was in tools/project.py, where print_category now reads fuzzy_match_percent from the measures dictionary and includes a fuzzy-match percentage in the progress output alongside matched and linked percentages. The PR body also notes a README.md merge conflict was resolved by keeping melee's upstream/deleted README state because the generic template README did not apply.

key_files.reason: Updated print_category progress output to include fuzzy_match_percent.
Mentioned in PR body as a merge conflict; melee kept the upstream/deleted version rather than adopting the generic dtk-template README.

what_changed: Merged latest changes from encounter/dtk-template template/main into melee master.
In tools/project.py print_category, added fuzzy_match_percent = measures.get("fuzzy_match_percent", 0).
Changed the category progress line from reporting only matched and linked percentages to reporting fuzzy, matched, and linked percentages.
Documented a README.md conflict resolution: kept melee's upstream/deleted version because the template README was generic and not applicable.
smart_moves: Used measures.get("fuzzy_match_percent", 0), matching the surrounding pattern for progress metrics and preserving compatibility if the metric is absent.
Kept fuzzy match reporting distinct from exact matched code and linked completion, avoiding ambiguity in progress output.
Explicitly documented the README.md conflict decision in the PR body, which helps future template-sync work understand why the generic template README was not accepted.
decomp_lessons: Template sync PRs can affect local decomp workflow tooling even when no game code changes.
When adding new progress metrics to project tooling, follow the existing measures dictionary convention and provide safe defaults.
Progress output should distinguish fuzzy matching, exact matching, and linked file completion because they represent different decomp milestones.
For dtk-template merges, document conflicts and preserve project-specific files when generic template content does not apply.
naming_conventions: Existing related metric keys retained: matched_code_percent, complete_code_percent, total_units, complete_units.
User-facing progress wording uses short labels: fuzzy, matched, linked.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic was changed.
The tooling now surfaces fuzzy match percentage, which can help track approximate decomp progress separately from exact matched code.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the PR context.
The only recorded explanatory text was the PR body describing the dtk-template merge and README.md conflict resolution.
